title: polygala vulgaris subvar. collina
intro: Polygala vulgaris subvar. collina, also known as the common polygala or dragon's mouth, is a flowering plant in the milkwort family that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic activities, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Polygala vulgaris subvar. collina include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which may help to protect against chronic diseases. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tincture, decoction, or infusion, and is also used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
